item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most of those are from the 2k contest, and stayed all the way to now. They are all brilliant articles, and their main files are splendid. The alien made them sound like horrid ideas. This could teach writers a lesson on how language affects an article's quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't take this the wrong way, but I feel like this article winning the 2000 contest would've been kind of unfair, since it relies on other entries for its content so much.
Still, though, outside of the contest, I think it's neat and funny. +1

item.title: Re: 2020 the year of SCP-2020
item.content:encoded: Object class: super-Keter Apollyon

item.title: Re: 2020 the year of SCP-2020
item.content:encoded: This year should be contained

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasnt sure about this because it seemed mean spirited. even if it is the joke is also on yourself, given that you created a cliche brain of an alien. cliche, right? still, I was comforted by the clarification that this was done affectionately, so although this is a controversial one you have my hesitant +1.

item.title: 2020 the year of SCP-2020
item.content:encoded: 2020 the year of SCP-2020

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP has aged like ranch dressing. -1

Had it been made as a -j SCP, I could see it lasting longer, but it's just a joke SCP based on a 6-year old contest that was wrongly put on the main list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hilariously done, and considerably a excellent read if you've been on the site for a while.
+1

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) due to the presence of "NC" in the image's license. As such, it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the most dangerous SCP I've ever seen. It has the ability to make you want to blow your brains out after talking to it for five minutes.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quick question: If he's talking to Dr. King, wheres the Appleseed reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a wizard, wait for it, from space. A space wizard. Guys. That's not the only part though. He's a cyborg. And he's with five, with five catgirls. And guess what the twist is? That's right. The catgirls? Also cyborgs.

I remember that (those?) SCP(s?), especially the tale series that came of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my God! I didn't realize that he was just referencing other SCPs 'till the third paragraph. This is just freakin' great. Funnier than some joke SCPs. Self-awareness is one of my favorite things when reading SCP articles, and this pulls it off in a way that's not too obvious or over the top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first, I thought this article is normal. However, after I read all article of 2000 contest, I think it's very good.
Upvote.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed the SCP even though I only understood 2 of its references. The tone of voice came out pretty funny in my head.

https://youtu.be/2Bd9t8mnk5w

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting. Since it seems he has something to do with Rockwell, I managed to develop an entire headcanon for him. So yeah the idea is neat and funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The absolute BOY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it slots in well with the rest of the mainlist. It has a different tone than most non-J articles, but it describes plausible SCP Foundation operating reasonably in a believable Foundationverse, and focuses on something whose existence is not particularly difficult to accept, compared to a lot of the 'serious' articles we have.

I actually headcanon it as a piece of anomalous art/satire that the Foundation unwittingly picked up from the middle of a performance piece it starred in. I enjoy writing and reading about the Foundation taking other people's jokes seriously, and making this a -J would take that fun away, imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty funny and a good take wrt the writing process, but I still really feel like this should be a -J.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: Well that's a super great message!

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2020 is a caricature of a science fiction fan stuck in an unproductive state of mind. The article highlights how disconnected actual science fiction writing is from its trope-laden, cliche+twist mentality. SCP-2020 has no meaningful understanding of how writing actually works, and thus can never get past the brainstorming stage despite having the base ideas for a number of very successful articles.

The point isn't that the articles it references are cliche, it's that people who think in terms of "is this cliche, what's the twist" don't get anything done.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded:
Not really an SCP. Without knowledge that there was a contest, its not that good. Its just someone calling successful SCP's cliche, thats it.

Teeechnically this was also in the contest at the same time, as a later entry; it's not like the contest was done and over with and then the author wrote this one.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded:
The joke isn't just that new people make mistakes, I'm pretty sure it also trying to say "the ideas in the 2000 contest where super cliche" and while I don't really agree with that, I upvoted because it did it in a neat way.

This is why you read the entire comment before you type children. :C

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: Thanks for bumping the thread!

I get the joke, but its not funny.
It takes too much time into "haha, look, new people make these mistakes, hah."
Its not much of an SCP. It would make more sense as a joke SCP though.

It uses other peoples work for its own humor, which is what ruined it. If you removed the ideas from other people, theres nothing else to this.
Not really an SCP. Without knowledge that there was a contest, its not that good. Its just someone calling successful SCP's cliche, thats it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I have no idea how I've missed that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Check the post immediately above yours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I dumb or forgetful or should I look for skips this references on the J list as well? Catgirls? Brain in a jar? Which are those?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"There's this facility, right? It was made by someone at some time in the past to, to bring the whole world back from the apocalypse." - SCP-2000

"Guys. I had another idea. Forget that one. This one, this one is a brain in a jar, that's a guy." - SCP-2099

"It's okay though, because I just got this awesome idea. There's a wizard, wait for it, from space. A space wizard. Guys." - SCP-2085

"Actually, no. Because I just got a better idea. Wait. It's an AI, right? An artificial intelligence on a computer." - SCP-2048

"Wait. Guys. Here's an idea for you, I just got this idea. There's a time machine, right? And it sends people to the future and then back to the present, and in the future, in the future everyone dies from an asteroid." - SCP-2003

"Okay, wait, but seriously. Better idea. Guys. There's this alien, right? Like they have in the movies, with the green skin and the big black eyes and everything." - SCP-2020

"Wait. Wait wait wait. Two words, just two words: Laser. Butt. Disease." - SCP-2001-J

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last one's a -j,and there might be one that isn't around anymore (not sure though), but otherwise yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are these real SCP's?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it light-heartedly pokes fun at other 2000 contest entries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …it's one big "take that" to bad entries. Uh-huh.

item.title: Re: Variant Classifications?
item.content:encoded:
Scantron has a thing where he appends a Latin suffix to standard object classes – hence "Euclid-exsequi". The Latin suffix is sort of a danger thing, like saying "Euclid-Cognitohazardous" or something like that.

Here, "exsequi" means "carry out" in Latin, hence explaining that the object is capable of independent action.

item.title: Variant Classifications?
item.content:encoded:
So, What is "Euclid-exsequi?"

I've seen other class modifiers from time to time. Are they explained anywhere?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am very interested in the 'laser butt disease'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is still a little funny after the contest, but its riffing made more sense during the contest itself.

…So I guess hindsight really is 2020. Zing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly. Without awareness of the contest, this is kinda crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like this somewhat, but not enough to upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ayy lmao indeed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What can I say? It made me laugh. What else is there in life?

OTOH…
On the one hand (yes, there are two hands within that one other hand), it should make me cringe because that poor alien guy has writer's block, something I have lived through for much too long in the still too recent past.
On the other hand (the second hand within that other hand, I mean - come on guys, pay attention!) it makes me feel proud of NOT having too many ideas for writing (be it SCP or not) because, um, yeah… some ideas are, er, not as good as others… ;>

Anyway. +1, no hesitation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will always love this. Such a simple idea, but so perfect.
Also, obligatory ayy lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everyone else's ideas for this contest are so dumb, haha

Cute. However, this isn't 4chan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article it composed entirely of metacomentary. That's all it is, meta. This article's meta has meta. Even my upvote has meta!

Anyway, I though that this was pretty cool and cute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idunno, but that's cliché, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
when did this hit +100

why did this hit +100

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Debating between a neutral or an upvote. It's certainly fun and all, but it rides on the meta a bit too much even for me. I guess had I read it before some of the other meta-themed articles (both in terms of -Js and mainlisters) I'd have liked it more.

item.title: Doesn't hold up on its own?
item.content:encoded:
I've seen the criticisms throughout the discussion here that this SCP only holds up as a parody of SCPs (or SCP wiki contests) or if the reader has read all of the SCPs referenced.
I'm a newbie who only recognized SCP 2000 in there, and still enjoyed the entry on its own.
Not to mention our own friend Isaac Asimov ("Izzy") was well-known for writing such stories ("The Immortal Bard" and "Left to Right" are two I'd recommend).

I think that having an argument about the place of this SCP is fair, but I have to agree with everyone who is saying that it belongs in the main list.
This is silly, sure- but so is "The Immortal Bard", which you can find in Asimov collections right alongside his stories about death and despair. It's fun, but it's not ridiculous, I suppose is my point. A llama delivery service this is not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its a stereotypical alien with knowledge (it shouldn't have) of the shady foundation holding it.
…
I can dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why do I love the idea of a creature that is a science fiction 'thing' writing weirder science fiction stories so much?

Just upvoting because that idea alone gets to me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. This. A thousand times this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It shouldn't be in the main namespace, because it's not at all serious. (Like this entry? It's an entry about entries? And it's about itself?)

Uhm, in the most frank manner I can only call this complete bullshit. We have the goddamn Toaster, infinite pizza, even HUMANS GO HOME as popular mainlist articles that are lighthearted/humorous. If you don't find it amusing, fine, but there is absolutely a space in the mainlist for amusing articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is tagged "Euclid" because it is sapient, and therefore it is unpredictable. The article will not be removed to a personal page unless it reaches -10, and judging from its current rating, it is unlikely to hit so low.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why is this completely harmless entity tagged Euclid? What does "Euclid-exsequi" mean?

It shouldn't be in the main namespace, because it's not at all serious. (Like this entry? It's an entry about entries? And it's about itself?)

It shouldn't be a -J, because it's not very funny. (Like this joke? It's overdone?)

It contains a few halfway decent ideas, but doesn't hang together. I suggest moving this entry out of the SCP namespace to a personal page. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this one amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guys. So like, there's this guy, on a website somewhere, right? And he's like looking for articles to upvote, and he sees this utterly hilarious one, I mean super funny, that makes him laugh milk of his nose. And then, he like totally upvotes it. Cliche right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this.

Because when you/I/others try to describe scips, this is exactly what it ends up sounding like.

"So like, there's this staircase, in a university somewhere, right? And it's like super-creepy because you can hear a kid crying for help, and lights don't work right so it's always dark, right? Only - the staircase never ends, and you never get to the kid, but there's this creepy face that just pops up behind you! Guys? Wait, no really, it's super scary … "

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I'm terribly sorry, but I really, REALLY dislike this article. I've looked at it from every angle, pretended it was just a -j, tried to look at it as an affectionate parody, but… it really doesn't sit well with me. It seems enough people like it enough to keep it in the positives, but… well, I'm afraid I can't make myself, in good conscious, upvote as well.

Big fan of your work, Scantron. Just not this one. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Okay guys. So there's this raspberry that's stuck on flypaper, right? WRONG! It's actually some weird dimension-hopping space guy, and he wants to learn about our world? Cliche, right? But get this: he starts telling all sorts of cool and crazy stories about his world! He seems really dumb, but he's so smart we don't understand him! So then this weird thing appears from nowhere and gobbles him up! Why, you ask? I'm not too sure myself, just thought it would be cool, y'know?"
